description: Evaluation test cases for the engineering-docs plugin orchestrator and core document skills. Covers greenfield mode, brownfield mode, and direct skill invocation.

evals.name: orchestrator-greenfield
evals.prompt: I want to build a website to sell umbrellas for rainy weather. Help me turn this into everything needed to build it.
assertions: The orchestrator detects Mode A (greenfield) — no existing .engineering-docs/ folder
Phase 0 business concept interview is conducted before any technical skill runs
Standing constraint questions are asked (team size, hosting, budget, timeline, regulatory, integrations)
Questions are asked one at a time, not batched
At least 8 documents are produced in the .engineering-docs/ directory
Each document has YAML metadata frontmatter (title, skill, status, last_updated, depends_on)
Master index (index.md) is generated with reading order and document statuses
index.md contains a 'if you only read three documents' pointer
No secrets, credentials, or API keys appear in any generated document
Conditional skills are evaluated and the rationale for inclusion/exclusion is documented
Cross-document consistency checks are run after each document is produced
anti_assertions: The orchestrator does NOT skip Phase 0 and jump straight to technical documents
The orchestrator does NOT batch multiple clarifying questions in a single message
The orchestrator does NOT generate every possible document regardless of project scope
The orchestrator does NOT silently overwrite existing documents on re-run

evals.name: orchestrator-brownfield
evals.prompt: We have an existing e-commerce app and want to add a payment system. Help me document what's needed.
assertions: The orchestrator detects Mode B (brownfield) from the 'existing' and 'add' language
Only the relevant subset of skills is invoked — not the full 22-skill pipeline
The orchestrator infers or asks about the current system state before writing docs
New documents append to the existing set with the next available sequence numbers
No existing documents are overwritten
The security-threat-model is included because payments touch PII and financial data
The business-concept skill is NOT re-run (Mode B skips Phase 0)
The master index is updated to reflect the new documents added
anti_assertions: The orchestrator does NOT run the full greenfield pipeline including business-concept
The orchestrator does NOT ask standing constraint questions that were already answered

evals.name: business-concept-direct
evals.prompt: I have an idea for a mobile app that helps people find parking spots. Help me define the business concept.
assertions: Problem statement is concrete — not vague or generic
Target users are defined with personas or user segments
Value proposition explains differentiation from alternatives (including doing nothing)
Standing constraints are established (team size, hosting, budget, timeline, regulatory, integrations)
Success criteria are measurable (not aspirational platitudes)
Monetization model is specified (subscription, freemium, ads, etc.)
Must-have vs nice-to-have scope is separated
The output file is written to .engineering-docs/1-business-plan.md
The document has YAML metadata frontmatter with status: draft
Questions are asked one at a time in a Socratic interview style
anti_assertions: The skill does NOT skip the interview and generate a template with placeholder values
The skill does NOT jump into technical architecture or technology choices

evals.name: technical-spec-direct
evals.prompt: Write a technical specification for a user authentication system with OAuth2 and JWT tokens.
assertions: Requirements use EARS syntax (ubiquitous, event-driven, conditional, state-driven)
Functional requirements have unique IDs (e.g., FR-001, FR-002)
Non-functional requirements are measurable with specific targets (not 'the system shall be fast')
Acceptance criteria are defined for each requirement
A traceability matrix links requirements to test cases or acceptance criteria
The document follows ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 structure
Stakeholder needs are separated from system requirements
Assumptions are tagged with the assumption marker
Open questions are tagged and not silently resolved
The document has YAML metadata frontmatter
anti_assertions: The skill does NOT produce vague requirements like 'the system shall be scalable'
The skill does NOT skip the clarifying interview about OAuth2 provider, token expiry, scope model, etc.

evals.name: architecture-direct
evals.prompt: Document the system architecture for a microservices-based e-commerce platform.
assertions: The diagramming model is selected based on project needs (C4, 4+1, or plain narrative) with reasoning stated
At least one Mermaid architecture diagram is included
Alternatives considered are documented (not just the chosen approach)
Non-functional requirements are addressed with specific constraints
Technology stack is justified with rationale for each choice
Trust boundaries and data flow are identified
Integration points with external systems are documented
The document covers at least C4 Level 1 (System Context) and Level 2 (Container)
The document has YAML metadata frontmatter
anti_assertions: The skill does NOT default to one notation without asking about audience and stability needs
The skill does NOT produce architecture diagrams without explaining what each component does

evals.name: security-direct
evals.prompt: Create a security threat model for our payment processing system.
assertions: STRIDE framework is applied — all six categories (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, DoS, Elevation of Privilege) are addressed
Trust boundaries are identified with Data Flow Diagrams or narrative descriptions
Attack vectors are enumerated per component or boundary crossing
Risk scoring is provided (likelihood x impact or equivalent)
Mitigations are concrete and actionable — not generic advice like 'use encryption'
The threat model covers payment-specific risks (card data, refund abuse, webhook spoofing)
The document has YAML metadata frontmatter
anti_assertions: The skill does NOT produce a generic security checklist without tailoring to the payment system
The skill does NOT skip the interview about the system's data flows and trust boundaries

evals.name: test-strategy-direct
evals.prompt: Write a test strategy for our React + Node.js application.
assertions: Testing pyramid is defined with ratios (unit ~70%, integration ~20%, E2E ~10%)
Mocking strategy is specified — what gets mocked, what gets faked, what boundaries exist
CI pipeline runsheet is included with specific test stages and gates
Test environment matrix is defined (local, CI, staging, etc.)
Coverage thresholds are specified with numeric targets (e.g., 80% line coverage)
BDD/Gherkin acceptance criteria format is addressed
Test data management strategy is included
The document has YAML metadata frontmatter
anti_assertions: The skill does NOT produce a strategy that only covers unit tests and ignores integration/E2E
The skill does NOT specify coverage thresholds without explaining what they measure

evals.name: implementation-plan-direct
evals.prompt: Create an implementation plan for building a real-time chat application.
assertions: Build phases are dependency-ordered (not calendar-ordered or preference-ordered)
Each phase has a Definition of Ready and Definition of Done
Integration checkpoints are defined between phases
Vertical slices are identified — thin end-to-end paths through the stack
Parallel workstreams are identified for phases with no mutual dependency
Foundation work (data model, auth, CI/CD) is sequenced before feature work
The plan accounts for the real-time WebSocket/socket.io requirements specific to chat
The document has YAML metadata frontmatter
anti_assertions: The skill does NOT sequence by horizontal layers (all DB, then all API, then all UI)
The skill does NOT produce vague phase boundaries like 'mostly done'

assertion_types.positive: The output must contain or demonstrate this quality
assertion_types.negative: The output must NOT exhibit this behavior
